arts.ttl: Sunderland v West Ham | Hope for Hammers fans if Potter bites the bullet
arts.sum: Finally there is football to talk about as the Premier League kicks off for West Ham this afternoon. Following weeks of the ebb and flow of moods over the transfer activity – and lack of it- which the club has undertaken, events on the pitch take centre stage. At least a temporary respite from the […] Source

arts.ttl: Sunderland vs West Ham United – Match preview and team news
arts.sum: Sunderland mark their return to the Premier League after an eight-year absence with a home fixture against West Ham United at the Stadium of Light on Saturday. Regis Le Bris’s side, promoted via the Championship play-offs, have undergone a significant squad rebuild with an eye on consolidation, while West Ham, under Graham Potter, seek to […] The post Sunderland vs West Ham United – Match preview and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Sunderland vs West Ham United – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Sunderland return to the Premier League this Saturday. They will host West Ham United in a significant opening fixture, ending an eight-year absence from the top flight. Fresh from their play-off success, Régis Le Bris has overseen a £130m squad transformation designed for survival. arts.ttl: West Ham XI vs Sunderland – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: West Ham United open their 2025/26 Premier League season away at promoted Sunderland on Saturday, aiming to set a positive tone at the Stadium of Light. After finishing 14th last term, Graham Potter’s team integrates new signings with a focus on improvement, facing a Sunderland side full of momentum. arts.ttl: Fan Focus: West Ham fan Dan is predicting a score-draw on Sunderland’s Premier League return!

arts.ttl: Sunderland and West Ham both have reasons to dread this opening fixture
arts.sum: Long awaited, the Premier League returns with West Ham fans the less enthusiastic about the forthcoming season: Two players brought in and two on ‘free’ transfers hardly qualifies as a ‘rebuild’. Something all Hammers fans will be aware of is the clubs dreadful opening-day form in the Premier League. Indeed, West Ham have come out […] Source

arts.ttl: "We have to accept that we will suffer" - Regis Le Bris previews the upcoming season ahead of West Ham clash
arts.sum: The Frenchman also added that this provided an opportunity for Dan Neil, Sunderland's captain last season, to develop. For us as a club, a squad, as a coach and as a manager, it's a chance with this new experience to grow and that is the same for Dan."
